name: Plunk Email API
what_it_does: Open-source transactional email platform with REST API for sending emails, managing contacts, and building event-driven email automation — written in TypeScript with self-hosted option and managed cloud, offering a lightweight alternative to Mailchimp or SendGrid.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (password resets, notifications) via simple REST API
Building event-driven email automation without per-email pricing at scale
Self-hosted email platform for privacy-conscious applications needing full data control
Contact management and segmentation for small to medium product teams
Triggering automated email flows from agent workflows via event API
not_for: Large-scale email marketing to millions (use SendGrid, Mailchimp)
Complex behavioral email sequences requiring advanced segmentation (use Klaviyo)
Teams needing enterprise email deliverability tooling and IP warming services
best_when: A developer or agent needs simple transactional email sending with open-source flexibility, self-hosting option, and generous pricing without per-email cost concerns.
avoid_when: You need enterprise email marketing, advanced segmentation, or proven deliverability at scale.

auth.notes: Secret API key from Plunk dashboard. Passed as Authorization Bearer token. Public key available for client-side event tracking. Single key per project — no scope granularity.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $8/month for 10,000 emails; self-hosted: completely free
pricing.notes: AGPL v3 open source — free to self-host. Cloud has generous free tier. Simple, transparent pricing. Early-stage product with active development. Good for startups and indie developers.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply on free tier; higher on paid

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API is straightforward with clear send/contact/event endpoints. JavaScript SDK simplifies integration. Webhook support for tracking email events. Smaller ecosystem than established providers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Email sends create new sends — no idempotency key support. Contact creation/update is upsert-style. Event tracking creates new event records per call.
known_agent_gotchas: Early-stage product — API may evolve; pin SDK version for stability
Domain verification required before sending — setup step agents cannot automate
Smaller deliverability reputation vs. established providers — monitor bounce rates
Self-hosted requires SMTP relay setup (AWS SES, Postmark) for actual delivery

security_score_components.security_notes: AGPL v3 open source — fully auditable. TLS enforced on cloud. API key auth. Self-hosted provides full data control. GDPR compliant. EU-based. Domain verification prevents abuse.
